title: HTML Tokenization, Tree Construction, goquery, and Parser Differentials · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs html tokenization, tree construction, goquery, and parser differentials from revision, authorization, seed, URL, robots decision, request, response bytes, tree identity, record provenance, frontier transition, goroutine owner, report row, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for budgeted Go HTML tree adapter after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: Choose streaming tokenizer or x/net/html tree from required context, memory, malformed-input behavior, and selector needs.
Construct goquery documents from admitted readers and preserve base, source, parser, and response identity outside selections.
Test implied elements, misnesting, tables, templates, foreign content, comments, duplicate attributes, and deep input.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
